title: Inhibition of ngly1 for the treatment of cancer
abstract: In one aspect, the present disclosure provides GlcNAc-Asn analogs of the formula (I): wherein the variables are as defined herein. In another aspect, the present disclosure also provides pharmaceutical compositions and methods of using the compounds disclosed herein. Additionally, the present disclosure also provides methods of treating cancer comprising inhibiting NGLY1.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is Claimed Is: A method of treating a cancer in a patient comprising administering to the patient a therapeutically effective amount of an inhibitor of N-glycanase 1 (NGLY1). The method of claim 1, wherein the cancer is melanoma, ovarian cancer, breast cancer, cervical cancer, endometrial cancer, colon cancer, pancreatic cancer, prostate cancer, liver cancer, renal cancer, lung cancer, glioma, and lymphoma. The method of claim 1, wherein the cancer is glioma, liver cancer, or melanoma. The method of claim 2, wherein the cancer is melanoma. The method of claim 1, wherein the cancer is not multiple myeloma. The method of claim 5, wherein the cancer is not a hematologic cancer. The method according to any one of claims 1-6, wherein the cancer is associated with the dysregulation of NGLY1. The method of claim 7, wherein the dysregulation of NGLY1 is upregulation of NGLY1. The method according to any one of claims 1-8, wherein the inhibitor of NGLY1 is a biological molecule. 0. The method of claim 9, wherein the biological molecule is an shRNA. 1. The method of claim 10, wherein the biological molecule is an shRNA selected from: 2. The method of claim 9, wherein the biological molecule is an antibody. 3. The method of claim 9, wherein the biological molecule is a peptide or peptiomimetic. 4. The method according to any one of claims 1-8, wherein the inhibitor of NGLY1 is a small molecule. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the small molecule is a compound or composition according to any one of claims 37-80. 16. The method according to any one of claims 1- 15 further comprising identifying a patient with a cancer which shows dysregulated expression of NGLY1. 17. The method according to any one of claims 1- 16, wherein the method further comprises a second cancer therapy. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the second cancer therapy is surgery, a second chemotherapeutic agent, a radiotherapy, or an immunotherapy. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the second cancer therapy is a second chemotherapeutic agent. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is an alkylating agent or an alkylating-like agent. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is cisplatin, dacarbazine, or temozolomide. 22. The method of claim 19, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is an agent which inhibits a protein. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is an agent which inhibits the proteasome. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is bortezomib. 25. The method of claim 22, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is not bortezomib or carfilzomib. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is not an agent which inhibits the proteasome. 27. The method of claim 22, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is an agent which inhibits BRAF or an agent which inhibits downstream signaling of MAPK. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the second chemotherapeutic agent is vemurafenib. 29. The method of claim 18, wherein the second cancer therapy is a radiotherapy. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the radiotherapy is X-ray therapy.
